results.title: 7 Best Amazon Wishlist Alternatives in 2026 (That Work With Any Store) | Farha
results.content:
Farha is a universal wishlist app that works with any online store. Paste a product URL and the app auto-fetches the image, title, and price. This is the exact functionality Amazon Assistant used to provide, but Farha adds features Amazon never had: anonymous gift reservations, group gifting, real-time gift updates, and multilingual support in English, French, and Arabic.

## 1. Farha -- Best overall Amazon alternative

Full disclosure: Farha is our product. I will be straightforward about what it does well and where it falls short. [...] Key features:

 Works with any online store worldwide. Paste any URL.
 Anonymous reservations prevent duplicate gifts while keeping the surprise.
 Group gifting lets multiple people contribute toward one expensive item.
 Real-time gift updates notify you the moment an item is reserved, someone chips in, or a wish is fulfilled.
 Browser extension for one-click adding while you shop.
 Gifters can view and reserve items without downloading the app.

Why it replaces Amazon Wishlist: Farha does everything Amazon Assistant used to do (save items from any store) plus everything Amazon Wishlist never did (reservations, group gifting, real-time gift updates). Learn more about how Farha works.

results.title: Farha vs. WishApp (2026 Comparison)
results.content:
Skip to content

WishApp LogoWishApp Logo

# Farha vs. WishApp (2026 Comparison)

Farha compared with WishApp, two wishlist apps side by side

Farha is a brand-new wishlist app from a solo founder, built around occasions and a few features WishApp doesn't have. Here is how the two stack up on price, the devices you can use, and the gifting tools that decide which one sticks.

Last updated: June 2026

Looking for a Farha alternative? Farha is a free, very new app with two real standouts: 24/7 price drop alerts and group gifting that WishApp doesn't match. But it's iOS-only today, with no Android and no browser extension. WishApp runs everywhere and is proven. Among Farha alternatives, WishApp is the one we'd pick.

## Feature comparison [...] ### Gifting and group features

Farha and WishApp split this one. Farha has two things WishApp doesn't: group gifting, where several people pool money toward one big item, and thank-you notes you send after the gift arrives. Both are thoughtful. WishApp goes wider instead, with collaborative lists where a couple can co-manage one wishlist, a follow system, a discover feed, and full reserved-and-received tracking with a note like 'from Sarah'. Both name Secret Santa as an occasion, but neither draws names automatically yet.

### Ease of use and trust [...] ### Sharing, privacy and reservations

Both apps get the core right. Share a list with one link, and friends open it in a browser with no account needed. Both hide reservations so the owner never sees who claimed what, which is the whole point of a gift list. Farha adds a nice touch here: it supports Arabic with full right-to-left layout, plus French, which very few wishlist apps bother with. WishApp answers with QR codes, all currencies, and a wider language reach on web, but no Arabic. For a MENA or francophone family, Farha is genuinely the friendlier option.

### Gifting and group features

results.title: Best Housewarming Gift Ideas: 40+ Gifts New Homeowners Actually Want (2026) | Farha
results.content:
Etiquette says no. The Emily Post Institute explicitly discourages housewarming registries, because housewarmings aren't gift-driven events. The polite alternative: keep a personal wishlist of things you need for the new place, and share it only when guests ask what to bring. That answers the question without demanding a gift.

Either at the party or within a few weeks of the move. Many etiquette guides actually recommend waiting two to three weeks after move-in, because by then the new homeowners know exactly what's missing. A slightly late housewarming gift is completely acceptable, and often more useful than an early one.

## Put these tips into practice [...] ### Is it OK to ask for housewarming gifts or make a registry?

Etiquette says no registry. The Emily Post Institute explicitly discourages housewarming registries because the event isn't gift-driven. The polite alternative: keep a personal wishlist of things the new place needs, and share it only when guests ask. See our guide to sharing a wishlist without awkwardness.

### When should you give a housewarming gift?

At the party, or within a few weeks of the move. Many etiquette guides actually favor giving two to three weeks after move-in, once the homeowners know exactly what's missing. Late is fine. A useful gift in week three beats a guessed one on day one.

## Conclusion [...] | Your relationship | Commonly cited range | Good fit |
 --- 
| Coworker or neighbor | $20–$40 | Candle, plant, local treats, dish towels |
| Good friend | $30–$50 | Cutting board, cheeseboard, tool kit, wine and snacks |
| Close friend or family | $50–$100+ | Quality cookware, robot vacuum fund, group gift |

Two more conventions worth knowing. Couples give one household gift, not two separate ones. And timing is flexible: giving at the party is standard, but arriving with a gift two to three weeks after the move is completely fine, and often better, because by then they know exactly what the house is missing.

results.title: 6 Best Wishlist Apps for Couples in 2026 (Tested and ... - Farha
results.content:
Yes. Farha, CoupleWish, and Giftster all work for year-round couple gifting — birthdays, anniversaries, Valentine's Day, or spontaneous gifts. Wedding-specific apps like MyRegistry are overkill for casual use and feel formal outside of formal events.

### 7 Best Free Wishlist Apps in 2026 (Honest Comparison)

10 min read [...] In Farha, no. Secret reservations hide claimed items from the wishlist owner. Other apps with this feature include Giftster and CoupleWish. Amazon Wishlist does not support this — gift givers can reveal themselves by accident.

Farha lets anyone view and reserve gifts from a shared link using a web browser — no app download or account needed for the viewer. Most other wishlist apps require both parties to sign up.

Farha supports English, French, and Arabic with full RTL layout — the only wishlist app with Arabic support. Giftful supports 9 European languages but does not include Arabic. [...] ## 1. Farha, best overall for couples

Farha is a universal wishlist app built for everyday gifting between partners, families, and friend groups. According to Giftster's 2025 growth data published on GlobeNewswire, 25% of gifting now happens off-calendar — spontaneous occasions outside of holidays and birthdays. Farha is designed exactly for this: the anytime gift, not just the annual event.

You add items from any website by pasting a link or using the browser extension. Farha pulls the product title, image, and price automatically. Share your list with your partner via a single URL — they can view it in a browser without signing up. When they reserve something, it's hidden from your view so the surprise stays intact.
